subject: Statistics
title: Overfitting: Memorizing Noise
description: A quick win: understand Overfitting: Memorizing Noise.

hook.text: A student memorizes every practice test answer but fails the real exam. That's overfitting.

buildup.text: A model that fits training data perfectly may capture noise instead of real patterns.

discovery.text: Overfit models have amazing in-sample performance but terrible out-of-sample predictions.

twist.text: More complex models are more prone to overfitting. The best model is often simpler than you think.

climax.text: The goal isn't to explain the past perfectly — it's to predict the future reliably.

punchline.text: A perfect fit to old data is a terrible fit to new data.

quiz.question: What is overfitting?
options: A model captures noise in training data and performs poorly on new data
A model is too simple to capture the real pattern
A model has too few predictors
